name: Jacques Derrida
core_thesis: Article-backed thinker stub created during primitives_v2 Phase 2 from `data/articles/source/derrida.md`.

cross_engagements.brief: Abhinavagupta presses the same epistemic authority axis in the epistemological register, but with the value `scripture-plus-transformative-experience` rather than `deconstructive-reading`.
Bannanje Govindacharya presses the same determination axis in the logical-dialectical register, but with the value `non-sublatable-difference` rather than `differential-deferral`.
Friedrich Nietzsche works the same method axis in the historical-genealogical register. Here the operative value is `genealogy`; Friedrich Nietzsche lands on that same value.
Akshapada Gautama presses the same method axis in the methodological register, but with the value `formal-proof-or-inference` rather than `deconstruction`.
Bhartrhari presses the same semantic mediation axis in the semantic-linguistic register, but with the value `language-as-creative-manifestation` rather than `language-differentially-defers-presence`.
Adi Sankara presses the same truth-structure axis in the epistemological register, but with the value `hierarchical-standpoint-truth` rather than `paraconsistent-or-both-held`.
Akalanka presses the same soteric end axis in the soteriological register, but with the value `isolation-or-discriminative-release` rather than `not-soteriological`.

engaged_works.title: De la grammatologie
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored modern monograph; bibliographic ascription is uncontroversial.
engaged_works.summary: Derrida's programmatic deconstruction of the phonocentric privilege of speech over writing in the Western philosophical tradition. The book traces the metaphysics of presence — the assumption that meaning is fully present to consciousness in the spoken word — through Plato, Rousseau, Saussure, and Lévi-Strauss, and argues that writing is not a derivative supplement to speech but the prior condition (*archi-écriture*) of any system of differential traces. The technical apparatus includes *différance*, the *supplement*, the *trace*, and the *pharmakon*. The work is the founding text of Derridean deconstruction. The comparative corpus reads its critique of presence-metaphysics as a structurally distinct (though sometimes superficially compared) move from the Advaita *adhyāsa*-doctrine: Derrida deconstructs presence as a metaphysical privilege without committing to any positive ontology, whereas Śaṅkara's critique of the *adhyāsa*-relation issues in the positive ontology of *nirviśeṣa Brahman* as self-luminous *cit*.
